title: How To Verify A Land Title Deed In Kenya Before You Buy
author: Esther Achieng
excerpt: A step-by-step guide to confirming a title deed is genuine and free of disputes before committing your money.

content.text: Buying land is one of the most significant financial decisions you will ever make, and title verification is the single most important step in protecting that investment. In Kenya, land fraud remains a real risk, but a methodical verification process can eliminate almost all of it.
Start With An Official Search At The Lands Registry

The first step is always a registry search, which confirms the registered owner, any existing encumbrances, and whether the parcel number actually matches the land being sold. This search can now be done online through the Ministry of Lands portal, though physical registry visits remain useful for older titles.
Confirm The Seller's Identity Matches The Title

Cross-check the national ID or company registration details of the seller against the name on the title deed. Any mismatch, however small, should pause the transaction until clarified through proper legal channels.
Physically Visit And Survey The Land

Never rely solely on paperwork. Engage a licensed surveyor to confirm the boundaries on the ground match the registered size and shape. This protects you from boundary disputes and encroachment issues that documents alone cannot reveal.
Work With A Qualified Conveyancing Lawyer

A conveyancing lawyer will handle due diligence, draft the sale agreement, and ensure the transfer is properly lodged with the registry. Their fee is a small price compared to the risk of an unverified transaction.
At PrimePlot Realty, every plot we list has already passed this entire verification process, so our clients can move forward with confidence rather than starting due diligence from scratch.

title: 5 High-Growth Areas To Invest In Land This Year
author: James Kariuki
excerpt: From Konza to Athi River, here are the corridors showing the strongest appreciation potential right now.

content.text: Land appreciation is rarely random — it follows infrastructure, employment hubs, and government investment. Here are five corridors we are watching closely this year.
Konza Technopolis
Continued government-backed development around the tech city is steadily pulling in residential demand from workers and contractors in the area.
Athi River
Industrial growth and improved road access have made this corridor increasingly attractive for commercial plot investors.
Kitengela
Affordable entry prices combined with proximity to Nairobi continue to drive strong residential plot demand.
Ruiru
Expanding road networks and university presence have made Ruiru a consistent performer for both residential and commercial plots.
Naivasha
Tourism, geothermal investment, and the inland port project make Naivasha a longer-horizon but high-potential investment area.

title: 7 Costly Mistakes First-Time Land Buyers Make
author: Samuel Mwangi
excerpt: Avoid these common pitfalls that cost new investors time, money, and sometimes their entire investment.

content.text: Every year, first-time buyers lose money on preventable mistakes. Here are the most common ones we see, and how to avoid them.
Skipping The Title Search
The single most expensive mistake is buying without an official search. It takes days, not months, and can save you from total loss.
Not Visiting The Site In Person
Photos can mislead. Always visit the actual plot, ideally with a surveyor, before transferring any funds.
Ignoring Zoning And Land Use Restrictions
A plot zoned for agriculture cannot simply be used for commercial development without the right approvals — verify this before buying.
Working with a licensed consultancy like PrimePlot Realty removes most of this risk, since due diligence is built into every listing we offer.

title: Residential vs Commercial Plots: Which Should You Buy?
author: James Kariuki
excerpt: Breaking down the returns, risks, and use-cases of each plot type to help you decide where to put your money.

content.text: Both plot types can build wealth, but they suit different goals, timelines, and risk appetites.
Residential Plots
Lower entry cost, steadier demand, and easier resale make residential plots a strong choice for first-time investors.
Commercial Plots
Higher entry cost but stronger long-term appreciation in growth corridors, particularly near transport and trade routes.

title: Understanding Land Rates And Annual Charges In Kenya
author: Esther Achieng
excerpt: What land rates are, how they're calculated, and what happens if they go unpaid for too long.

content.text: Land rates are annual charges levied by county governments on registered land, and unpaid rates can eventually lead to legal action against the title.
How Rates Are Calculated
Rates are typically based on the unimproved site value of the land as assessed by the county valuation roll.
Consequences Of Non-Payment
Accumulated unpaid rates can attract penalties and, in extreme cases, can be a barrier to completing a future sale until cleared.

title: Smart Ways To Finance Your Next Land Purchase
author: Linda Cherono
excerpt: From SACCOs to flexible installment plans, explore the most practical ways Kenyans are funding land purchases.

content.text: Not everyone buys land in cash, and that's perfectly fine — there are several practical financing routes worth considering.
SACCO Land Loans
Many SACCOs offer land-specific loan products with more flexible terms than conventional bank mortgages.
Developer Installment Plans
Buying directly through a consultancy offering installment plans, like PrimePlot Realty, lets you secure a plot while spreading payments over time.
